title: Week 5 Hands On Activity Automated Data Exploration INFS3822
body:
INFS3822 – AI for Business Analytics



Week 5 Hands-On Activity – Automated Data Exploration
Pre-requisites
Please complete all Week 5 Pre-Seminar Activities, available on Moodle, under Week 5 section.

Step by step guide
Open your project on SAS Viya Model Studio (refer to Week 5 Hands-On Activity - Create an ML project
in SAS Viya to create a project)
You need to indicate which variable is your Target variable (to be predicted).
Select the variable salary_in_usd and change its Role to “Target”.




You can also drop some variables if you think they are not needed. For example, given that the target
variable is salary_in_usd, we do not need to keep the variables salary and salary_currency.
                                                                                                        CRICOS Provider Code 00098G




Page 1
 Update the project settings.




Update the Data Partition settings for the proportions of training, validation, and test data.
     o   Training Set is used to train the model
     o   Validation Set is used to tune the model (adjust the model configuration)
     o   Test Set is used to evaluate the final model.




                                                                    The Partition Data proportions can be
                                                                    edited after creating a project, but only
                                                                    before the Data node is run. After
                                                                    the Data node is run, you can view the
                                                                    partition proportions, but you cannot edit
                                                                    them until you reload the data source.




Common Proportions (training-validation-test) are:
     o   70-15-15 Split: Balanced datasets where you want a good amount of data for training while still
         having sufficient data for validation and testing.
     o   80-10-10 Split: Used when the dataset is large enough that 10% is still a substantial amount of
         data for validation and testing.



Page 2
      o   60-20-20 Split: Used when you want more data for validation and testing, possibly to ensure that
         the model is not overfitting and to have more reliable performance metrics.


Click on the Pipelines tab.




Add a Data Exploration node.




Page 3
 Set the input data partition to “All data” and the variable selection criteria to “Importancec”. Then,
run the pipeline.




Once the execution of the pipeline is completed, open the Data Exploration results by right
clicking on the Data Exploration node and selecting “Results”.




Page 4
 Explore the Data Exploration results.




In-Class Discussion about the results of the Data Exploration node.




Page 5
